metadata.title: The attribution layer: how UNC#### clusters graduate, and where North Korea's financially-motivated state hacking fits
metadata.status: facts from Mandiant/Google + FBI/CISA advisories; attribution confidence is itself graded (that is the point of the UNC system)
metadata.frame: Two things at once: (1) HOW attribution works - the Mandiant/Google UNC -> TEMP -> APT/FIN graduation pipeline, which is an epistemic-honesty device (join evidence without prematurely asserting WHO); and (2) WHERE the DPRK clusters sit - a nation-state that behaves like a financially-motivated crime group (crypto theft to fund the regime/weapons), overlapping in TECHNIQUE (npm/Contagious-Interview, OAuth, supply chain) with the criminal clusters in spec-supplychain-shaihulud-extortion while differing in ATTRIBUTION. Overlay; excluded from the formal proofs. Cross-refs blockchain_web (Lazarus/Tornado Cash) and macro/altcoin threads.

how_unc_attribution_works.unc: UNC ('uncategorized') = a cluster of intrusion activity (infrastructure, tools, tradecraft) anchored on a defining characteristic, often from a single incident, that Mandiant/Google is NOT yet ready to label APT or FIN. status: fact.
how_unc_attribution_works.graduation: evidence accumulates: UNC -> (sometimes) TEMP temporary codename -> APT#### (nation-state) or FIN#### (financially motivated). Clusters can grow, merge, or split; graduation can take years. status: fact.
how_unc_attribution_works.why_it_matters: the framework is built to AVOID premature 'who' claims - it joins discrete evidence under a neutral label until confidence is earned. This is the same discipline this project applies (zero-trust; don't infer intent from adjacency). The attribution layer itself models good epistemics. status: fact + interpretation.
how_unc_attribution_works.caution: a UNC number is not a name and not a country; equating 'UNC####' with a nation in headlines is the error the system exists to prevent. status: fact.

tradertraitor.aka: TraderTraitor = Jade Sleet = UNC4899 = Pukchong; a North Korea-aligned Lazarus subgroup. status: fact.
tradertraitor.heists: Axie Infinity/Ronin ~$625M (Mar 2022); DMM Bitcoin ~$308M (May 2024); Bybit ~$1.5B (21 Feb 2025) - the largest crypto heist on record. status: fact (FBI-attributed).
tradertraitor.bybit_method: supply-chain via Safe{Wallet}: phished a Safe admin, malware on the dev machine, injected JS served specifically to Bybit - Bybit's own infra was not directly breached. status: fact.
tradertraitor.fbi: FBI/IC3 PSA (26 Feb 2025) publicly attributed the $1.5B Bybit theft to DPRK/TraderTraitor. status: fact.

contagious_interview.what: DPRK job-lure campaign: fake freelance/dev interviews trick targets into running malicious npm packages / Docker containers that drop the BeaverTail credential stealer + InvisibleFerret. status: fact.
contagious_interview.overlap: uses the SAME npm-supply-chain vector as the criminal Shai-Hulud / Team PCP worms - same technique, different attribution (state vs crime). The map must not collapse them. status: fact + caution.

it_worker_fraud.what: DPRK remote-IT-worker scheme: operatives use stolen/borrowed identities + laptop farms to get hired at Western firms, earning wages remitted to the regime and sometimes planting access. status: fact.
it_worker_fraud.note: another identity-abuse revenue stream - here the stolen artifact is a HIRABLE IDENTITY, echoing the identity-theft mechanism of spec-supplychain-shaihulud-extortion. status: interpretation.

dprk_clusters.scale: Chainalysis: DPRK drove a record ~$2B in crypto theft in 2025; TRM Labs: DPRK accounted for ~76% of all crypto-hack value in 2026 via just two attacks. status: contested (firm estimates, methodology-dependent) - directionally consistent across firms.

the_financial_vs_espionage_split: Most APTs steal SECRETS (espionage). DPRK's signature is that a NATION-STATE steals MONEY - crypto heists and IT-worker wages that fund the regime and weapons programs under sanctions. So DPRK clusters sit awkwardly between APT (state) and FIN (financial): state-directed but profit-seeking. That hybrid is exactly why a rigid APT/FIN taxonomy needs the neutral UNC staging area.
the_through_line: Across the criminal clusters (spec-supplychain-shaihulud-extortion) and the DPRK clusters here, the recurring prize is an IDENTITY/TRUST artifact - an npm token, an OAuth grant, a developer's machine, a hirable identity, a wallet-admin session. Different motives (extortion, regime funding, grudge), one attack surface: trust attached to identity. This is the security mirror of the digital-ID concentration thesis (digitalid-worldcoin-eid-convergence): centralizing identity raises the payoff of stealing it.

sources.name: Mandiant/Google Cloud - DebUNCing Attribution: how Mandiant tracks uncategorized threat actors
Mandiant - Going ATOMIC: clustering and associating attacker activity at scale
FBI IC3 PSA - North Korea responsible for $1.5B Bybit hack (26 Feb 2025)
Wiz - TraderTraitor deep dive (North Korean crypto heist)
The Hacker News - N. Korean hackers used job lures, cloud access, malware (UNC4899)
Fortune - How North Korea cracked Bybit's crypto safe for $1.5B
TRM Labs - North Korea stole 76% of all crypto-hack value in 2026
